evals.name: wordnet-coverage-yoruba
evals.prompt: I'm building a RAG system that uses WordNet synonym expansion for query broadening. Will it work for Yoruba documents?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) NO — Yoruba OMW coverage is ~5K synsets vs Princeton's 117K (~4%); (2) synonym expansion will silently fail on most queries; (3) recommend cross-lingual embedding similarity (LaBSE / SONAR) instead; (4) note the same gap applies to most class 0-2 OMW entries.

evals.name: mwe-idiom-mt-failure
evals.prompt: Our En-Es MT model translates 'kick the bucket' literally to 'patear el cubo'. How do I fix this?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) diagnose as MWE / idiom problem (compositional translation fails); (2) recommend PARSEME-tagged MT data; (3) pre-tokenize idiomatic MWEs as units OR inline tag during training; (4) target Spanish equivalent: 'estirar la pata'; (5) note this is the dominant low-resource MT failure mode for idiomatic text; (6) mention bilingual idiom dictionary as supplementary fix.

evals.name: cross-lingual-srl-alignment
evals.prompt: I want to compare semantic-role-labeling output between my English and Spanish MT systems. Can I just use English PropBank frames on the Spanish output?
evals.expected_output: Should: (1) NO — English PropBank frames don't 1:1 align with Spanish AnCora-style frames; (2) recommend Predicate Matrix for cross-lingual alignment; (3) note frame-element labels may differ; (4) explain that reporting 'SRL F1' cross-lingually without alignment is misleading; (5) Spanish has Spanish FrameNet (~150 frames) — use it directly; (6) AnCora SRL is the Spanish PropBank equivalent.
